nodes.text: Oh, you scanned it? Is something — did I smudge it? The barcode thing? I can clean it off if that's the issue.

responses.text: It scanned fine. Just a routine check.
I need to confirm some details with you.

nodes.text: Oh thank god. Yeah, I was worried I'd messed it up somehow. Okay. Yeah. Ask me whatever you need.

responses.text: Can you confirm your date of birth?

nodes.text: Sure. Lisinopril, ten milligrams, Dr. Webb — Marcus Webb, NPI and everything, it's all on the prescription. I've been with him about three years now. Good guy.

responses.text: Prescription checks out. Just need your date of birth.

nodes.text: Yeah — it's the lisinopril. Ten milligrams. I'm supposed to take it every morning but I forget sometimes when I'm on a job. Doc says it's not the end of the world as long as I'm consistent most days.
January eighth, eighty-nine. Capricorn, apparently, though I have no idea what that means.

responses.text: That matches. You're all set, Tommy.

nodes.text: Great. Thanks for being quick about it — I still gotta grab that drink and then I'm done for the day.
